ip_outcome_note: Iroha is a named, specific FFXI character introduced in Rhapsodies of Vana'diel (2015). The card's type line 'MOBIUS XI' and job 'Samurai' match her FFXI profile exactly. Her signature naginata with flame detail, white-and-red martial outfit, and long hair are consistent with canonical FFXI art. Vision pass description is a direct match to her canonical visual identity. Confident verify.

trivia_bullets.text: Iroha is the main heroine of Final Fantasy XI: Rhapsodies of Vana'diel (2015), the narrative finale of FFXI's first fourteen years. She is a Hume, daughter of Tenzen and Kagero, chief priestess of the Reisen Shrine on Reisenjima — a Far East locale — and arrives from a doomed future to warn Vana'diel of approaching annihilation.
sources: WebSearch: Iroha FFXI Rhapsodies of Vanadiel

trivia_bullets.text: Iroha's FFTCG card type line reads 'MOBIUS XI' — meaning her card art and character data are drawn from the Mobius Final Fantasy version of FFXI collaboration content rather than a direct FFXI source. Her job is listed as Samurai in the card. In FFXI she wields a naginata and uses samurai job skills, a profile confirmed by the FFXIclopedia and Gamer Escape wikis.
sources: FFTCG Crystarium Opus VIII Limited Review

trivia_bullets.text: Iroha (8-004R) is a 3-cost, 7000-power Fire Forward with Samurai job. Her passive increases all damage Fire characters deal to opposing Forwards by 1000, and her dull ability deals 1000 damage to a chosen Forward. The FFTCG Crystarium Opus VIII limited review rated her 3.5/5, calling her 'absolutely nuts' and 'a huge draw to fire when drafting/deckbuilding' due to how she amplifies burn combos alongside Sabin, Cloud, and other fire forwards with damage abilities.
sources: FFTCG Crystarium: How to Win Limited Opus VIII Review

trivia_bullets.text: Iroha cross-appeared in Final Fantasy XIV via 'The Maiden's Rhapsody' collaboration event, first in 2015 (Patch 3.1 / Heavensward), repeated in 2017, 2020, 2022, and 2024 — one of the longest-running XIV crossover events on record. In XIV she appears as an Iroha-type NPC Hyur Midlander with Lancer class, asking for directions to Selbina.

related_cards.name: Sabin
related_cards.note: Cited as a key combo synergy partner with Iroha's damage-amplification ability

related_cards.name: Cloud (VIII)
